_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
AFIR imposes obligations on **EU Member States**, not on transport operators or freight forwarders. Member States must ensure the infrastructure targets are met within their territory by the specified deadlines.

The workspace's position in the compliance chain is as follows:

| Layer | Entity | AFIR Obligation |
|---|---|---|
| Regulator | European Commission | Monitors Member State progress; may take infringement action |
| Obligated entity | EU Member States | Must deploy infrastructure to meet mandatory targets |
| Operational dependency | Road carriers (hauliers) | Rely on Member State-deployed infrastructure to operate zero-emission vehicles commercially |
| Workspace position | Freight forwarder | No direct AFIR obligation; commercial exposure through carrier capability and client sustainability expectations |

The workspace sits outside the AFIR compliance chain as a regulated subject. Its exposure is operational and commercial, not regulatory.

**Legal Confirmation Required:** The workspace's exposure under any client contract clauses that commit to zero-emission or low-carbon road transport delivery on specific European corridors should be reviewed by legal counsel in light of the AFIR infrastructure deployment timeline and the HDV CO₂ standards phasing. If the workspace has signed or is negotiating contracts with sustainability performance commitments tied to road transport mode, the achievability of those commitments depends on AFIR infrastructure being in place on the relevant corridors by the committed date.

*Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/1804, Article 3 (national targets, Member State obligations), EUR-Lex, 22 September 2023. https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1804/oj/eng*
